text.title: Competitor Analysis Examples
text.summary: Two worked examples (note-taking app, email marketing) and a common-mistakes table showing the correct fix for each failure mode.

example.note: Market: personal knowledge management. Competitors analysed: Notion, Obsidian, Roam Research (direct); Apple Notes, Google Docs (indirect); AI writing tools (future).
example.code:
| Competitor | Strength | Weakness | Opportunity | |------------|------------------|-----------------------|-----------------------| | Notion | All-in-one | Slow, complex | Speed, simplicity | | Obsidian | Local-first | Steep learning curve | Easier onboarding | | Roam | Backlinks | Expensive, dated UI | Better UX, lower price|
Positioning: "For busy professionals who want fast, simple notes with linking. Unlike Notion, we're single-purpose and lightning fast. Unlike Obsidian, we work great out of the box."
example.note: Gap chosen: experience gap (speed and simplicity). Messaging names specific competitors to make the claim credible.
Gap discovered through pricing analysis: all tools price by subscriber count, creating early paywalls for solopreneurs under 1000 subscribers.
example.code: Opportunity: "Generous free tier (5000 subscribers), solo-focused features, no team complexity" Gap type: Price gap + audience gap — no tool focuses on sub-1000 subscriber solo operators.
example.note: Price gap and audience gap combined. Competitor pricing analysis identified the tier ladder, not just features.

antipattern.description: Analysing only direct competitors and omitting indirect and future types.
antipattern.reason: Indirect competitors are what customers actually use today; future competitors are the real threat in 18 months. Missing both produces a false sense of whitespace.

antipattern.description: Surface-level analysis limited to homepage copy without digging into pricing pages, reviews, and feature documentation.
antipattern.reason: Pricing footnotes (annual lock-in, seat minimums, transaction fees) and 1-star review themes contain more actionable signal than marketing copy.

antipattern.description: One-time analysis with no refresh schedule.
antipattern.reason: A competitor matrix is stale within 60-90 days. Pricing, feature flags, and team size change frequently. Schedule quarterly re-runs with the same template to produce a diff.

antipattern.description: Copying competitors' features rather than differentiating on their weaknesses.
antipattern.reason: Feature parity produces table stakes, not a positioning story. Your differentiation is their weakness as expressed in customer complaints.
